hub.title: GitFreddo
hub.subtitle: Open, clone, or create a git repository to get started.

openFolder.title: Open a folder
openFolder.description: Browse for a local folder containing a .git directory.

init.title: Initialize a new repository
init.description: Create a new git repository in an empty or new folder.

clone.title: Clone a repository
clone.description: Clone from GitHub, GitLab, or any git remote.

createGithub.title: Create on GitHub
createGithub.description: Create a new repository on GitHub and clone it locally.

createBitbucket.title: Create on Bitbucket
createBitbucket.description: Create a new repository on Bitbucket and clone it locally.

startup.title: Welcome to GitFreddo
startup.intro: GitFreddo is free and community-driven. Here are quick ways to support development and stay current.
startup.supportBody: If GitFreddo is useful to you, consider buying me a coffee to help fund ongoing work.
startup.newsEmpty: No news items yet. Check back after the next update.
startup.updatesBody: Use the app update banner to download and install the latest GitFreddo release when it becomes available.

ai.intro: AI assist fills commit and stash messages from your staged or working-tree changes, and can auto-resolve merge conflicts. Supports OpenAI-compatible endpoints (LM Studio, Ollama, OpenAI, OpenRouter).
ai.baseUrlHintApi: OpenAI-compatible API base URL (/v1 is appended automatically).
ai.apiKeyHint: Stored locally in your GitFreddo settings file on this machine.
ai.modelPlaceholder: Leave empty to use the first available model
ai.systemInstructionsDesc: General behavior for all AI calls.
ai.systemInstructionsPlaceholder: You are a helpful git assistant…
ai.commitInstructionsDesc: Style and format for commit messages.

github.title: GitHub
github.description: Connect your GitHub account to browse repositories, open pull requests, and manage remotes.
github.tokenHint: OAuth opens your browser for sign-in. Personal access tokens need repo scope and are stored only on this machine.
github.waitingAuth: Waiting for authorization in your browser…
github.sshKeyMissing: No GitFreddo SSH key found on this account.

fork.title: Fork repository
fork.description: Create a fork under your GitHub account, then clone it locally.
fork.connectFirst: Connect GitHub in Settings → Integrations to fork repositories.

repo.name: Repository name
repo.connectFirst: Connect GitHub in Settings → Integrations to create repositories.

bitbucket.title: Bitbucket
bitbucket.description: Connect Bitbucket Cloud to browse repositories, open pull requests, and manage remotes.
bitbucket.tokenHint: OAuth opens your browser for sign-in. App passwords need repository access and are stored only on this machine. SSH key upload requires App password (Bitbucket blocks OAuth for that API).
bitbucket.sshRequiresAppPassword: SSH key upload needs an app password connection. Disconnect, then reconnect with App password.
bitbucket.waitingAuth: Waiting for authorization in your browser…
bitbucket.sshKeyMissing: No GitFreddo SSH key found on this account.

maintenance.updatesDesc: GitFreddo checks GitHub Releases for new versions when the app starts.
maintenance.intro: Scan for unreachable commits and stale refs, then prune unreachable objects.
maintenance.openRepoFirst: Open a repository to run maintenance.
maintenance.unreachableDesc: Commits not reachable from any branch or tag.
maintenance.confirmPruneMessage: This will permanently remove {{count}} unreachable commits.
maintenance.settingsBackupDesc: Export or restore GitFreddo preferences, AI configuration, and integration logins. Backup files may contain API keys and tokens — store them securely.
maintenance.confirmImportMessage: This replaces your current GitFreddo settings with the values from the backup file. Integration tokens included in the backup will also be restored.

repoFiles.connectRequired: Open a repository to edit repository files.
repoFiles.missing: {{file}} does not exist in this repository yet.

repoHooks.connectRequired: Open a repository to manage git hooks.
repoHooks.description: View, edit, enable, and disable git hook scripts.
repoHooks.empty: No hooks found in the hooks directory.
repoHooks.deleteConfirm: Delete hook "{{name}}" and all its variants?

integrations.intro: Connect external services to GitFreddo for authenticated git operations and future features.

sidebar.openRepoPrompt: Open a repository to browse branches and stashes.
sidebar.branchClickHint: Click to focus commit · Double-click to checkout
sidebar.forceDeinitSubmoduleMessage: Force deinitialize submodule at "{{path}}"? Local checkout will be removed.
sidebar.removeSubmoduleMessage: Remove submodule at "{{path}}" from this repository?
sidebar.forceRemoveWorktreeMessage: Force remove worktree at "{{path}}"? Uncommitted changes will be lost.
sidebar.deleteRemoteConfirm: Remove remote "{{name}}"? Remote-tracking branches will be removed from this repository.
sidebar.openRepoForPr: Open a repository to view pull requests.
sidebar.connectForge: Connect GitHub or Bitbucket in Settings → Integrations.
sidebar.notLinkedGitHub: This repository is not linked to GitHub.
sidebar.notLinkedBitbucket: This repository is not linked to Bitbucket.
sidebar.notLinkedForge: This repository is not linked to a supported forge.
sidebar.openRepoForIssues: Open a repository to view issues.
sidebar.title: Title

tools.listenGitTitle: Capture git command output in the Git log
tools.gitLoggingOff: Git logging is off. Turn on Listen to capture git command output.

createTag.title: Create tag
createTag.lightweightHint: Leave empty for a lightweight tag, or add a message to create an annotated tag.

mergeParent.description: Choose the mainline parent for merge commit {{hash}}.

checkoutRemote.title: Checkout remote branch
checkoutRemote.description: Create or checkout local branch tracking

pickaxeSearch.title: Pickaxe search
pickaxeSearch.placeholder: Code or text to find in history

bisect.title: Git bisect
bisect.activeHint: Bisect is active. Mark commits as good or bad, then reset when finished.
bisect.inactiveHint: Start bisect with a known bad commit and optionally a known good commit.

fileHistory.title: History — {{path}}
fileHistory.noCommits: No commits found for this file.
fileHistory.selectCommit: Select a commit to view its changes.

rebaseSequence.title: Interactive rebase
rebaseSequence.mergeWarning: Selection includes merge commits. Interactive rebase may fail or produce unexpected results.
rebaseSequence.editTodo: Edit the rebase todo list. Base commit:
rebaseSequence.emptyTodo: Rebase todo list cannot be empty.

mergeBranch.title: Merge {{branch}}
mergeBranch.conflictsOne: Merge started with conflicts in {{file}}. Resolve them to continue.
mergeBranch.conflictsMany: Merge started with conflicts in {{count}} files. Resolve them to continue.

forcePush.title: Force push?
forcePush.message: The remote has commits that are not in your local {{branch}}. Force pushing will overwrite {{remote}} with your local branch.

timeline.graphSubtitle: Commits and branches · click to inspect
timeline.openRepoPrompt: Open a repository to view commits.

workingTree.openRepoPrompt: Open a repository to view changes.
workingTree.discardChangesOne: Discard local changes to "{{path}}"? This cannot be undone.
workingTree.discardChangesMany: Discard local changes to {{count}} files? This cannot be undone.
workingTree.removeFromRepoMessage: Remove "{{path}}" from the repository and stage the deletion?
workingTree.deleteFileMessage: Delete untracked file "{{path}}"? This cannot be undone.
workingTree.proposedCommitPlanDescription: Commits are ordered so each step stays self-contained and should not break functionality.
workingTree.stageBeforeCompose: Stage files before composing commits with AI.
workingTree.cleanUntrackedDescription: Permanently remove untracked files and directories from the working tree. This cannot be undone.
workingTree.proposedCommitsDescription: AI grouped your staged changes into {{count}} commit. Review the messages below, then create them all or stage one group in the commit panel.
workingTree.proposedCommitsDescription_plural: AI grouped your staged changes into {{count}} commits. Review the messages below, then create them all or stage one group in the commit panel.

diff.noDiffContent: No diff content for this file.
diff.selectAllLinesTitle: Select all {{label}} lines for this conflict

conflicts.openRepoPrompt: Open a repository to view merge conflicts.
conflicts.aiProposalsReady: AI proposals ready for {{count}} file — open to review
conflicts.aiProposalsReady_plural: AI proposals ready for {{count}} files — open to review
conflicts.someFilesStillHaveMarkers: Some files still contain conflict markers.
conflicts.aiProposalsReadyReview: AI proposals ready — review each file before saving.

detail.selectCommitOrChanges: Select a commit or uncommitted changes.
detail.mergeCannotCherryPickGroup: Merge commits cannot be cherry-picked as a group
detail.selectionNotContiguous: Selected commits must be adjacent in history
detail.selectFileForDiff: Select a file to view its diff
detail.deleteHeadDescription: Remove the latest commit on the current branch by resetting to its parent.
detail.dropDescriptionOne: Permanently remove this commit from branch history via interactive rebase. Later commits will be replayed without it.
detail.dropDescriptionMany: Permanently remove these commits from branch history via interactive rebase. Later commits will be replayed without them.
detail.revertDescription: Creates a new commit that undoes the changes from {{hash}}. Safe for shared branches because history is not rewritten.
detail.remoteWarning: This branch is {{count}} commit ahead of its remote. Rewriting history may require a force push. Prefer Revert when the commit is already on the remote.
detail.remoteWarning_plural: This branch is {{count}} commits ahead of its remote. Rewriting history may require a force push. Prefer Revert when the commit is already on the remote.
detail.commitOrStashBeforeRewrite: Commit or stash your uncommitted changes before rewriting history.
detail.finishOrAbortGitOp: Finish or abort the current merge, rebase, or cherry-pick first.
detail.resetSoftDescription: Removes the commit but keeps all changes staged.
detail.resetMixedDescription: Removes the commit and unstages changes; files remain in your working tree.
detail.resetHardDescription: Removes the commit and discards all changes permanently.
detail.rewordDescription: Rewrites the message for {{hash}} and replays later commits on this branch. Requires a clean working tree.
detail.mergeCannotReword: Merge commits cannot be reworded from here.
detail.commitOrStashBeforeReword: Commit or stash your changes before rewording.
detail.removeStaleDescription: Commits that still appear in the graph but are not on your current branch are kept alive by git references — local branches, backup refs from rebases (refs/original/…), tags, or remote-tracking branches. Delete those references, then Git can remove the commits permanently.
detail.pasteCommitHashes: Paste commit hashes, one per line
detail.commitsNotOnBranch: {{count}} commit exist in the repository but not on your current branch.
detail.commitsNotOnBranch_plural: {{count}} commits exist in the repository but not on your current branch.
detail.noStaleReferences: No stale references found. If commits still appear in the graph, try Settings → Maintenance → Scan for stale commits to remove reflog-only objects.
detail.noFileChangesInStash: No file changes in this stash.

fork.title: Fork repository
fork.description: Create a fork under your GitHub account, then clone it locally.
fork.connectFirst: Connect GitHub in Settings → Integrations to fork repositories.

repo.connectFirst: Connect GitHub in Settings → Integrations to create repositories.
repo.name: Repository name

repoPicker.connectFirst: Connect GitHub in Settings → Integrations to browse repositories.

fork.title: Fork repository
fork.description: Create a fork under your Bitbucket account, then clone it locally.
fork.connectFirst: Connect Bitbucket in Settings → Integrations to fork repositories.

repo.connectFirst: Connect Bitbucket in Settings → Integrations to create repositories.
repo.name: Repository name
repo.nameRequired: Workspace and repository name are required

repoPicker.connectFirst: Connect Bitbucket in Settings → Integrations to browse repositories.

contextMenu.rebaseAtHead: Rebase current branch (already at this commit)
contextMenu.removeStaleHistorySpansBranches: Remove stale branch history (selection spans branches)…

update.repoNotFound: Could not check for updates at github.com/{{repo}}. The repository may not be published yet, may be private, or may have no releases.
update.authFailed: Update check failed because GitHub rejected the request. If the release repository is private, sign in to GitHub in GitFreddo settings.
update.networkFailed: Could not reach GitHub to check for updates. Check your network connection and try again.
update.waitForGitOperations: Wait for git operations to finish before restarting
